title: Devices and methods to create a protective gas cushion
abstract: Devices and methods for creating a protective gas cushion in an outwardly open volume which are useful in surgery are provided. More particularly, the invention relates to a device (10) having a flexible hose portion (30) having an intake end and a discharge end and a distal tip portion (40) connected to the discharge end of the flexible hose portion. The distal tip portion comprises a rigid, porous polymer body (50) having a pore size of 7 to 45 Î¼m. The distal tip portion (40) is adapted to be positioned in the volume and the device (10) is arranged to supply the gas to the volume through the rigid, porous polymer body (50), the rigid, porous polymer body being arranged to supply the volume with the gas in a substantially laminar, continuous flow to enable the formation of the protective gas cushion intended to fill the volume and prevent air from the environment from reaching the volume.
claims:
Claims 1. A device arranged to create a protective gas cushion in an outwardly open volume, the device being connectable to a gas source, the device comprising: (i) a flexible hose portion having an intake end and a discharge end and (ii) a distal tip portion connected to the discharge end of the flexible hose portion, the distal tip portion comprising a rigid, porous polymer body having a pore size of 7 to 45 pm, wherein the distal tip portion is adapted to be positioned in the volume and wherein the device is arranged to supply the gas to the volume through the rigid, porous polymer body, the rigid, porous polymer body being arranged to supply the volume with the gas in a substantially laminar, continuous flow in order to enable the formation of the protective gas cushion intended to fill the volume and thereby prevent air from the environment from reaching the volume. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is hydrophobic. 3. The device of claim 1, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is made of high density polyethylene. 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body comprises a hollow interior portion. 5. The device of claim 4, wherein the distal tip portion comprises a rigid, non- porous polymer body proximal of the rigid, porous polymer body. 6. The device of claim 1, wherein the flexible hose portion comprises a filter to remove impurities from the gas. 7. The device of claim 1, wherein the flexible hose portion has a length and comprises a malleable wire for at least portion of its length. 8. The device of claim 1, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is arranged to supply the gas in several directions from the rigid, porous polymer body. 9. The device of claim 1, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body has a pore size of 25 to 45 pm. 10. A system comprising a gas source and a device, the device being arranged to create a protective gas cushion in an outwardly open volume, the device being connectable to a gas source, the device comprising: (i) a flexible hose portion having an intake end and a discharge end and (ii) a distal tip portion connected to the discharge end of the flexible hose portion, the distal tip portion comprising a rigid, porous polymer body having a pore size of 7 to 45 pm, wherein the distal tip portion is adapted to be positioned in the volume and wherein the device is arranged to supply the gas to the volume through the rigid, porous polymer body, the rigid, porous polymer body being arranged to supply the volume with the gas in a substantially laminar, continuous flow in order to enable the formation of the gas cushion intended to substantially fill the volume and thereby prevent air from the environment from reaching the volume, and the intake end the flexible hose portion being connected to the gas source. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is hydrophobic. 12. The system of claim 10, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is made of high density polyethylene. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body comprises a hollow interior portion. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the distal tip portion comprises a rigid, non-porous polymer body proximal of the rigid, porous polymer body. 15. The system of claim 10, wherein the flexible hose portion comprises a filter to remove impurities from the gas. 16. The system of claim 10, wherein the flexible hose portion has a length and comprises a malleable wire for at least portion of its length. 17. The system of claim 10, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is arranged to supply the gas in several directions from the rigid, porous polymer body. 18. The system of claim 10, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body has a pore size of 25 to 45 pm. 19. The system of claim 10, wherein the gas comprises a majority of carbon dioxide. 20. A method for creating a protective gas cushion in an outwardly open volume comprising: providing a device arranged to create a protective gas cushion in an outwardly open volume, the device being connectable to a gas source, the device comprising: (i) a flexible hose portion having an intake and a discharge end and (ii) a distal tip portion connected to the discharge end of the flexible hose portion, the distal tip portion comprising a rigid, porous polymer body having a pore size of 7 to 45 pm, wherein the distal tip portion is adapted to be positioned in the 

 volume and wherein the device is arranged to supply the gas to the volume through the rigid, porous polymer body, the rigid, porous polymer body being arranged to supply the volume with the gas in a substantially laminar, continuous flow in order to enable the formation of the gas cushion intended to substantially fill the volume and thereby prevent air from the environment from reaching the volume; attaching the intake end of the flexible hose portion to a gas source; positioning the distal tip portion in the volume; supplying the gas to the volume through the device in such a way that a substantially laminar, continuous flow of the gas is formed, wherein the positioning of the distal tip portion in the volume and the supplying of the gas to the volume are performed so that the controlled gas flow forms the gas cushion which substantially fills the volume and thereby prevents air from the environment from reaching the volume. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is hydrophobic. 22. The method of claim 20, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is made of high density polyethylene. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body comprises a hollow interior portion. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the distal tip portion comprises a rigid, non-porous polymer body proximal of the rigid, porous polymer body. 25. The method of claim 20, wherein the flexible hose portion comprises a filter to remove impurities from the gas. 26. The method of claim 20, wherein the flexible hose portion has a length and comprises a malleable wire for at least portion of its length. 27. The method of claim 20, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body is arranged to supply the gas in several directions from the rigid, porous polymer body. 28. The method of claim 20, wherein the rigid, porous polymer body has a pore size of 25 to 45 pm. 29. The method of claim 20, wherein the outwardly open volume adjoins a portion of the body of a living organism, the portion of the body being a portion that is normally not exposed to the atmosphere. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the living organism is a human. 31. The method of claim 20, wherein the gas comprises a majority of carbon dioxide.
